payload.text:
This is a revamping and building of the JK PALMS and Gardens Website.

So this is a Park and Resort Garden Center. It also has a football pitch, Court Yard and a Palm Yard Garden. 
I have created the site before now, but then I think it was deemed time to upgrade and beautify it again. https://jkpalmsandgarden.com

I will describe how the structure and layout should be then go ahead to describe the design and all others.

Home Page - About, Events, Services, Contact Us, Book Now

On the Home Page: After the hero section we would have a section for features and stuff, so it will just talk about the park and resort center with like 3 pictures. 
Then the next section will be our services, in there our time of operation, what we offer, and all others
next is gallery which can have neat grid layout style I will add thte pictures needed. the pictuers will have microanimations, and on hover zoom
then testimonial section and finally the footer with all contact details and socials icon. 

For the various other pages mentioned previously that'll be on the navbar, yes they should still have their own page if clicked, then they will have home on their navbar so u can go back to the home page. then since the main purpose of the site is to handle the bookings of users or visitors to the park center, we would have some Call to actions button especially on the hero section. 
this booking page has a complex structure, but for now we can just do a mock up. SO it collects the following data, Name, Phone, Email, Then u select the venue u want to book for we have Courtyard, Football Pitch, Palm Garden, Kids Playground, then based on where u select the pricing will automatically come up and it is in naira. then except for football booking, if the user select the others it will collect number of guest the children and then adult. A date stuff to select the date they're booking or something like that. then a message box beneath incaser they have anything to say or let us know. aside the football pitch other venues are booked for a whole dahy, while football pitch is booked per time in a day, depending ont he duration u want to play. min is 1 hour and max is 2hr 30mins. So the complex part id that they will be a calendar stuff so if u try to book a date that someone has booked it will say this date is booked and then u can see the calendar to see available dates, then for football pitch it will be by time and date also, so even if u book today if someone has that time u cannot book it, and then for instance a match os to end 1:30PM u cannot book immediately 1:30PM till whenever because the time to cklear of pitch or field will take a while, so we will gie a 20 mins interval if a time has been booked and someone tries to book immediately.

For the Design, I want Glass Morphism style and theme. I want light color design, but it can have a toggle switch at the navbar to switch to dark mode, but default is white. 	WIth teh glassy style and then neat and well organized layout. 3D movement animations where necessary, to increase the design appearance. It should be highly appealing and attracting to users and consumers. Make use of gradient colors rather than using solid colors all the time, and then use mathing fonts. In all be creatuve and work to perfection while keeping organization, user experience and neatness as priority.

Book Your Perfect Event in Just a Few Steps!
Welcome to our seamless booking system! Whether you're planning a thrilling football match or a relaxing day in our lush Palm Gardens or elegant Courtyard, we've made it easy for you. Here's how it works:

Enter Your Details: Fill in your name, phone number, email, and any special requests in the message box to let us know your needs.
Choose Your Location: Select from our three stunning venues:
Football Pitch: Perfect for action-packed games, booked by the hour for your convenience.
Palm Gardens: Ideal for all-day events, surrounded by tropical beauty.
Courtyard: A charming space for full-day gatherings with a touch of elegance.
Select Duration (Football Pitch Only): If you choose the Football Pitch, pick your desired hours (1, 1.5, 1.75, or 2). For Palm Gardens or Courtyard, the entire day is yours!
Check Availability: Click "Show Calendar" to view booked dates. Click any date to see specific times (for Football Pitch) or full-day bookings (for Palm Gardens/Courtyard). The calendar uses color codes: Yellow for Pending bookings (awaiting admin approval), Light Red for Booked (approved by admin), and no color for Available slots. Use the Previous and Next buttons to navigate through days.
Pick Your Date & Time: Choose your start time using the date picker. For Football Pitch, the end time adjusts based on your duration. For full-day venues, it’s set to cover the entire day.
Review & Submit: See the total price, confirm your details, and hit "Submit Booking" to reserve your spot!
Ready to create unforgettable memories? Start booking now!


u don't need to copy exactly the format of the previous, i just want u to get info and data from there

**Considering the Project Scope**

I'm currently focused on the initial phase. I've broken down the project: it's a website revamp for "JK Palms and Gardens." This involves understanding their diverse offerings – park, resort, garden center, football pitch, courtyard, palm garden, and a kids playground. This preliminary analysis forms the bedrock for subsequent design decisions.


**Developing the UI Concept**

I'm now fully immersed in the design direction. I've landed on glassmorphism with a light mode default and a dark mode toggle. Gradient colors and 3D animations are integral. The structure will be clean and highly appealing. I'm leaning heavily on React 18+, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS. Lucide React will provide the icons, and Framer Motion handles the animation. I'm keeping Recharts as a backup.


**Mapping the Architecture**

I've sketched out the project's directory structure now. The key files are `index.html`, `index.tsx`, `App.tsx`, and the other initial supporting modules. I am now prioritizing the architecture for the UI components, which encompass `Navbar.tsx`, `Hero.tsx`, `Features.tsx`, and so on. Also, the `ui/` directory with reusable components is starting to take form.


**Prioritizing Core Features**

I've just been thinking about the booking process now. I'm focusing on handling different venues like the football pitch versus others. I'll need state variables for the details, and price calculation. I'm moving toward a step-by-step implementation strategy for the setup, core files, constants, UI components, and the main page components. I'm starting with setup and metadata, then moving onto defining the `Venue` and `Booking` types. The UI will have a `GlassCard` wrapper for consistent styling.


**Connecting the Dots**

I'm now linking all the various components together, ensuring seamless navigation with `HashRouter`. I'm also focusing on refining the glassmorphism aesthetic, starting with a background container in `App.tsx` and incorporating CSS gradients. I'm leveraging `picsum.photos` for image sourcing and `framer-motion` for animations. I'm also adding in the specific requirements, such as the 20-minute buffer for the football field, the naira currency, and the contact details, to ensure the mockup stays as true to the specifications as possible.


**Optimizing for Practicality**

I've just been thinking about the calendar functionality. Instead of building a complex calendar from scratch, I've opted for a simplified approach. I'll use a standard date picker for input and then visually represent available "slots" in the mockup. This keeps the code size down and still demonstrates the UI well. I'm also ensuring all imports are at the top level and using the Tailwind CDN, React 18's `createRoot`, and verifying `useEffect` hooks for infinite loops. I'm also double-checking image sources and trying to pick images that fit the context.

**1. Home Page Updates (`Home.tsx` & `constants.ts`):**
*   **Hero Section:** Removed the floating "Safety First" animation block on the image as requested.

*   **Services Section:** Updated to include "Event Hosting", "Garden Parties" to reflect the specific offerings of the park. Added an "About" section using your provided text and ensuring navigation IDs (`id="about"`, `id="services"`, etc.) are in place so the navbar links work correctly.
*   **Gallery:** Implemented a Lightbox (modal) that opens when you click the "VIEW" button or the image itself, allowing users to see the full-size image.
*   **Clean Up:** Removed the "Meet Our Team" and "Blog" sections.

payload.text:
**Designing Backend Interaction**

I'm now focusing on connecting the front-end booking form with the PHP backend. I've been given some pre-existing scripts, including `send.php` and `db_conn.php`, that provide the foundational logic. I'm keen to adapt these to the form submission. I'm taking a look at the data format and what it needs to send to the backend. I have the database credentials now, so connecting the front-end to a backend is within reach.


**Defining the Frontend's Role**

I'm now focusing on creating the React component that will simulate the PHP backend interaction. Given the constraints, I will design a `Booking.tsx` component to POST data to an endpoint like `api/send.php`.  I'll ensure this reflects the expectations of the provided PHP scripts.  The goal is to produce the client-side code and backend scripts, leaving the user to set up the server. I have incorporated the user's requirement to send emails using `bookings@jkpalmandgarden.com`.


**Implementing Database Schema**

I'm now figuring out the data tables I will need in the database. Per the user's explicit request, I will generate the SQL necessary to create the required tables. I'm focusing on adapting the existing PHP scripts, specifically `send.php` and `db_conn.php`, to handle these new tables. The user explicitly stated I should use the given database credentials. I'm modifying the PHP script to return JSON responses for the booking requests to align with the new React implementation.


**Converting Data for Backend**

I'm now adapting the PHP script `send.php` to return JSON responses, enabling seamless error handling in the React frontend. I'm focusing on ensuring data compatibility between the React form and the PHP script. Specifically, I'm mapping the React state variables to the PHP-expected POST variables. I am transforming the date and time data into the "start" and "stop" format for the database interaction. I'll make the `start` datetime field based on date and time, and calculate the `stop` time based on the duration.


**Generating Server-Side Files**

I am now focusing on generating the PHP files (`db_conn.php`, `send.php`) and SQL schema (`database.sql`) to be provided to the user.  I'll ensure these files leverage the provided credentials for database interaction. The React component (`Booking.tsx`) is almost ready. I'll make the PHP script strictly an API, taking booking details and inserting/emailing them.  I'm noting that the React component will calculate the `price` and determine the `slot`. I am removing any old logic, like 14 hours check. I'll prioritize updating the PHP validation to be more general.


**Validating & Finalizing Components**

I'm now focusing on finalizing the PHP files, specifically the `send.php` script and the SQL schema. The `bookings` table has been defined with columns for `name`, `phone`, `email`, `slot`, `message`, `start`, `stop`, `price`, `duration`, `status`, and `booking_code`. My simulation reveals the core data flow is complete: the React form submits data via `fetch`, `send.php` processes it, connects to the database, performs validations (overlap checking), inserts the data, and sends an email. I'll make sure to provide the PHP files with the necessary libraries included. The user has provided an email address for booking notifications, and I'll keep the `status` column as a placeholder for later use. I have a checklist with a 5/5 confidence rating for completion.


**Adapting to Existing Code**

I'm now integrating the user's previously used code, assuming PHPMailer is accessible, or providing a `composer` instruction. I've updated `Booking.tsx` to use `fetch` and map React state to PHP POST fields. New files `api/send.php`, `api/db_conn.php`, and `database.sql` are being created to streamline the API. I'm keeping the POST data format consistent for easy integration. I'm focusing on validating the start/stop times.


**Calculating Datetime Parameters**

I'm currently finalizing the PHP script's datetime handling and table schema. I've focused on generating `start` and `stop` values in the appropriate ISO format (`YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss`) for the database. Specifically, I'm considering scenarios for venues, like "Courtyard" bookings, to ensure correct calculation and overlap prevention. The table has been updated to use specific values, like `VARCHAR` for `slot`, and `DATETIME` for `start` and `stop`.


**Developing Complete API Functionality**

I'm now in the process of finalizing the API structure, aiming to ensure it's easy to deploy and use. I'm focusing on the integration of the React frontend with the PHP backend. I'll provide clear instructions on how to deploy the files, given the requirement for them to reside in a public directory, like `public/api/`. I will assume PHP support and focus on a smooth integration process. My next step will be to prepare documentation for the user on how to deploy the solution.

Here is the specification and code implementation for the functional booking system.

### Specification for Functionality Upgrade

1.  **Backend Integration (PHP)**:
    *   We will create a structured API directory (`public/api/`) to house the backend logic.
    *   **`db_conn.php`**: Contains the database connection using the credentials provided.
    *   **`send.php`**: A modernized version of your previous script. instead of returning HTML, it will return **JSON**. This allows the React frontend to stay open and display a nice success modal without reloading the page. It will handle the database insertion and email sending logic.
    *   **SQL Schema**: I will provide a `database.sql` file containing the table structures needed for the system to work.

2.  **Frontend Update (`Booking.tsx`)**:
    *   The `handleSubmit` function will be updated to calculate exact `start` and `stop` times based on the user's selection.
    *   It will use the `fetch` API to POST this data to `api/send.php`.
    *   It will handle "Loading", "Success", and "Error" states to give feedback to the user.

3.  **Data Mapping**:
    *   **Football Pitch**: Time is specific. `start` is the selected date+time. `stop` is start + duration.
    *   **Full Day Venues (Courtyard/Palm Garden)**: The system will automatically set the time to 08:00 AM (Start) and 10:00 PM (End) for the selected dates to ensure the overlap logic works correctly in the database.

### Database Setup (SQL)

Run this SQL on your database (`jkpalman_mydb`) to create the necessary tables.

```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bookings (
    id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    phone VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    email VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    slot VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    message TEXT,
    start DATETIME NOT NULL,
    stop DATETIME NOT NULL,
    price DECIMAL(10, 2) NOT NULL,
    duration FLOAT NOT NULL,
    status VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'pending',
    booking_code VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS cms_content (
    id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    content_key VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    content_value TEXT
);

-- Insert default CMS values needed for the PHP script
INSERT INTO cms_content (content_key, content_value) VALUES 
('contact_email', 'bookings@jkpalmandgarden.com'),
('email_greeting', 'Thank you for choosing JK Palms and Garden.'),
('email_footer', 'Best Regards,\nManagement'),
('background_image', 'https://jkpalmandgarden.com/img/bg.jpg');
```

### Code Implementation

diagnostics.message: Property 'slot' does not exist on type 'BookingRecord'.

payload.text:
**Debugging the Booking Issue**

I'm currently trying to tackle a type error in `AdminPanel.tsx`. The compiler is complaining that `BookingRecord` lacks a 'slot' property. A quick peek at `types.ts` reveals `BookingRecord` extends `BookingState`, so I need to check if that `BookingState` type is missing this property. This is a crucial step to eliminate the type error.


**Analyzing Type Definitions**

Okay, I've got it. I was so focused on `BookingState` that I didn't see where the problem actually lay. Looking at the component and the types, I was barking up the wrong tree. The `BookingRecord` type *doesn't* have a 'slot' property, and it's being used directly in the AdminPanel component on line 173 where it references `booking.id`. The AdminPanel is expecting something in the booking object which is not defined in `BookingRecord`.


**Addressing the Type Mismatch**

Right, I've tracked down the root cause. It's a type mismatch issue; the `booking` object within `AdminPanel` on line 171 is of the `BookingRecord` type, which doesn't include the 'slot' property. The 'slot' property comes from somewhere else within the mapped data in `fetchBookings`, and I must make sure that `BookingRecord` contains that property. Now I'm focused on the necessary type adjustments in `types.ts`, and how to ensure the `BookingRecord` includes all the properties needed by the AdminPanel.


**Mapping Data Structures**

Now, I'm analyzing the data flow and the structure of incoming data. It looks like the API response includes a `slot` property, which is then mapped in `fetchBookings`.  This missing `slot` is what's causing the type error. I'm considering three possible fixes. Either updating the `BookingRecord` or `BookingState` type to include an optional `slot` property, casting to a different type directly in `AdminPanel.tsx` where it is used, or mapping the `slot` to `venue` during the initial data transformation.  I'm now weighing which approach makes the most sense.


**Implementing Data Transformation**

I've been examining the discrepancies between the API response structure and the expected data by the `BookingRecord` type. The database stores the value as `slot`, while the component expects `venue`. This type mismatch is causing the runtime error. I've concluded that the most elegant solution is to perform the mapping of `slot` to `venue` directly in the `fetchBookings` function, where the data is initially processed, before passing into `AdminPanel.tsx`. This avoids type casts, and fulfills the type requirements of `BookingRecord` and `BookingState`. I will now modify the function to fix the data structure.


**Integrating Data Transformation**

I've been reviewing the code. The problem is that the `AdminPanel` expects the property `venue`, but the data from the API response uses `slot`. My latest idea is to ensure that the data from the API maps `slot` to `venue` directly within the `fetchBookings` function. I'll modify the `mappedData` in `AdminPanel.tsx` to handle `slot` and rename it to `venue`, so that the typing of `BookingRecord` can be honored. I am confident this resolves the error and maintains type safety.


**Implementing Venue Mapping**

I've been working on a data transformation to resolve the type issue. Now, I'm going to ensure the mapping of the `slot` property to `venue` happens consistently. I'm focusing on the `AdminPanel.tsx` component. I'm going to alter the `fetchBookings` function to map `slot` from the database to `venue`, and cast the value to `VenueType`, so the code accesses `booking.venue` correctly. Then, I'll update the JSX to use `booking.venue`. I'm confident this will solve the type discrepancy.


**Finalizing Venue Property**

I'm now implementing the change in `AdminPanel.tsx` and am confident I've identified the optimal solution. The primary issue stems from how the `slot` data from the backend is handled within the frontend code. I've decided to prioritize data integrity and type safety, and the most pragmatic approach is to perform data transformation within the `fetchBookings` function to map `slot` to `venue`. By removing the conditional operator, `booking.slot || booking.venue`, on line 173 and updating the type, the error should go away.

**Considering Backend Alternatives**

I'm currently focused on transitioning away from the PHP/MySQL approach. The idea of using Supabase is gaining traction, and I'm actively exploring the implications of this shift for my React application. It's a significant change, so I'm carefully evaluating the steps involved.


**Shifting Backend Strategies**

I'm now fully immersed in the Supabase integration. I've pinpointed the necessary client installation and initialization steps, along with configuration details. My focus has shifted to revising data access methods in `Booking.tsx` and `AdminPanel.tsx` to align with Supabase queries. A crucial piece is defining the required Supabase table structure for this implementation, which I need to clearly articulate for their setup.


**Refining Supabase Integration**

I've outlined concrete steps for the Supabase transition. I'm focusing on creating `lib/supabaseClient.ts` to manage the Supabase connection. My attention is now on modifying `Booking.tsx` and `AdminPanel.tsx` to integrate with Supabase, moving away from PHP. This means implementing `insert`, `select`, and `update` logic using the newly established client. I also need to verify and possibly adapt the existing types in `types.ts` to match Supabase's data structure. Finally, I'll clean up the React code.


**Structuring Supabase Interactions**

I'm now fully immersed in implementing Supabase queries. In `Booking.tsx`, I'm constructing JSON objects from input, ensuring they align perfectly with the `bookings` table schema, especially mapping 'slot' to 'venue'. I'm also adding error handling to check Supabase's `{ data, error }` response. In `AdminPanel.tsx`, I'm crafting `fetchBookings` and `handleUpdateStatus` functions using Supabase's `select` and `update` methods.


**Implementing Supabase Logic**

I'm now fully immersed in coding the Supabase interactions. I'll start by crafting `lib/supabase.ts`, then adjust `Booking.tsx` and `AdminPanel.tsx` to use it. Importantly, I need to include checks to gracefully handle the situation if the Supabase keys aren't set up yet. I'll need to update `index.html` to add `@supabase/supabase-js` to the import map, ensuring smooth integration.


**Initiating Supabase Implementation**

I've revised the plan to address the build issues. I'll begin by modifying `index.html` to include `@supabase/supabase-js` in the import map. Next, I'll create `lib/supabase.ts`, setting up the client with placeholder keys. Then, I'll update `Booking.tsx` to implement Supabase's `insert` method and generate unique booking codes. In `AdminPanel.tsx`, I'll implement Supabase's `select` and `update` methods. The design remains untouched. The "Processing" button will now wait for Supabase, and the Admin panel might display a "Connect Supabase" message if needed.

To switch from PHP/MySQL to Supabase, we need to:

1.  **Add the Supabase SDK** to your project imports.
2.  **Initialize the Supabase client** in a new file.
3.  **Update the Booking component** to write to Supabase.
4.  **Update the Admin Panel** to read from Supabase.

### **Important: Database Setup**
You will need to create a project at [supabase.com](https://supabase.com), create a table named `bookings`, and add the columns listed below. Then, copy your **Project URL** and **Anon Key** into the `lib/supabase.ts` file created below.

**Table Schema (SQL for Supabase Query Editor):**
```sql
create table bookings (
  id uuid default gen_random_uuid() primary key,
  created_at timestamp with time zone default timezone('utc'::text, now()) not null,
  booking_code text unique,
  name text,
  email text,
  phone text,
  venue text,
  start_time timestamp with time zone,
  end_time timestamp with time zone,
  duration numeric,
  guests_adults int,
  guests_children int,
  price numeric,
  message text,
  status text default 'Pending'
);
```

I have added the email confirmation functionality using **EmailJS**.

Here are the changes made:
1.  **Updated `index.html`**: Added the `@emailjs/browser` library to the import map.
2.  **Updated `components/Booking.tsx`**:
    *   Imported `emailjs`.
    *   Added logic inside `handleSubmit` to trigger an email after the booking is saved to the database.
    *   **Important**: I have used placeholder keys (`service_jk_palms`, `template_booking`, `YOUR_PUBLIC_KEY`). You must sign up at [EmailJS.com](https://www.emailjs.com/), create a service and template, and replace these placeholders in the code with your actual credentials for the emails to actually send.

The app will now attempt to send an email when a booking is made. If the keys are not configured, it will log a warning to the console but the booking will still proceed.

diffs.replacement:
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2020",
    "useDefineForClassFields": true,
    "lib": ["ES2020", "DOM", "DOM.Iterable"],
    "module": "ESNext",
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "moduleResolution": "bundler",
    "allowImportingTsExtensions": true,
    "resolveJsonModule": true,

    "jsx": "react-jsx",

  },

}

**Investigating TypeScript Errors**

I'm now zeroing in on the `GlassButton` component definition. The core issue revolves around the `className` prop within `GlassButtonProps`, even though `HTMLMotionProps<"button">` and its parent interfaces *should* include it. I'm focusing on potential type conflicts arising from the default prop value assignment (`className = ''`). Maybe that's the issue here!


**Analyzing Prop Inheritance**

I'm now investigating potential conflicts in the `GlassButton` prop inheritance. It appears the TypeScript error might be due to a discrepancy between the expected `className` prop from `HTMLMotionProps<"button">` and its actual availability during destructuring. I'm also examining the `framer-motion` types to pinpoint where the type definition for `className` might be getting lost or shadowed.

I've been thinking about the `GlassButtonProps` interface and its inheritance again, and it appears the issue might stem from the inheritance of HTML attributes from `framer-motion`'s `HTMLMotionProps`. The error `Upload failed: new row violates row-level security policy` occurs because **Supabase** protects your storage buckets by default, preventing unauthorized uploads.

Since you are uploading as a "guest" or "admin" from the frontend (without logging into Supabase Auth specifically), you must explicitly allow **public/anonymous** uploads in your Supabase Dashboard. I cannot fix this in the code directly because it is a security setting on your Supabase project.

I have updated the `AdminPanel.tsx` code to catch this specific error and show you a helpful popup with the exact instructions when it happens.

**To fix the error immediately, follow these steps in your Supabase Dashboard:**

1.  **Go to Supabase Dashboard** > **Storage**.
2.  Click on your **`images`** bucket (create it if it doesn't exist and make it "Public").
3.  Click on the **Configuration** or **Policies** tab for that bucket.
4.  Click **New Policy** > **For full customization**.
5.  Name it: `Allow Uploads`
6.  Allowed operations: Check **INSERT**.
7.  Target roles: Select **anon** (and **authenticated** if you want).
8.  Click **Review** and **Save**.

Now your uploads will work!

Here is the updated code which includes the helpful error message:

To ensure real-time updates work reliably across devices, I am introducing a **Sync Status indicator**, **Immediate Saving** for images (skipping the delay), and a **Polling Fallback** mechanism.

The polling fallback is crucial: even if Supabase Realtime (WebSockets) is blocked or misconfigured, the app will now automatically check the database every 10 seconds to pull new changes.

### Updates:
1.  **`context/DataContext.tsx`**:
    *   Added `syncStatus` to track if data is Saved, Saving, or Error.
    *   Modified `persist` to support **immediate saves** (used for images so they save instantly).

    *   Added better error logging for Row Level Security (RLS) issues.

2.  **`components/AdminPanel.tsx`**:
    *   Added a **Cloud Sync Status** indicator in the top right. It will show "☁️ All Changes Saved" or "⏳ Saving...".
    *   If it says "⚠️ Sync Error", it means your database needs the RLS policies fixed.

The error `column site_content.value does not exist` confirms that your Supabase database table `site_content` is missing the required column to store the data. It likely only has a `key` column, or the column is named differently.

To fix this, I am switching the code to use a column named `content` (which is safer than `value` as it avoids SQL keyword conflicts) and adding error reporting so you can see exactly what's wrong in the Admin Panel.

**CRITICAL STEP: You must run this SQL in your Supabase SQL Editor to fix the database structure:**

```sql
-- 1. Reset the table to ensure correct structure
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS site_content;

-- 2. Create table with 'key' and 'content' columns
CREATE TABLE site_content (
  key text PRIMARY KEY,
  content jsonb
);

-- 3. Enable Security (RLS)
ALTER TABLE site_content ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

-- 4. Create Policies to allow the app to Read and Write
CREATE POLICY "Allow Public Read" 
ON site_content FOR SELECT 
USING (true);

CREATE POLICY "Allow Public Write" 
ON site_content FOR INSERT 
WITH CHECK (true);

CREATE POLICY "Allow Public Update" 
ON site_content FOR UPDATE 
USING (true);
```

Here are the code updates to match this new structure:
